title: NodeOperatorBinding v1
description: Machine-readable schema for a node-held operator-assurance certificate. The binding is a bundle over `capability-passport.v1`: the operator participant issues a `node-primary-operator` passport to the target node, and the node signs a separate acceptance proving that it accepts that participant as primary operator. Derived node assurance remains an eligibility gate, not reputation.

binding/id.description: Stable identifier of this node/operator binding bundle.

binding/status.description: Lifecycle state of this binding bundle. Passport expiry or revocation should drive this projection.

passport.description: Full `capability-passport.v1` artifact issued by the operator participant. In this profile it is the participant-side consent claim: I agree to be primary operator of this target node.

passport_id.description: Stable identifier of the underlying operator-consent passport.

node_id.description: Target node receiving the primary-operator consent passport.

capability_id.description: Virtual capability profile used for node/operator binding. It is not a generic runtime execution permission.

operator/role.description: Operator role carried by this v1 binding. The first version models only the primary operator.

operator/attestation-ref.description: Reference to the operator participant's identity-assurance, Proof-of-Personhood, or federation attestation record.

operator/attestation-kind.description: Optional classifier for the attestation reference used to derive node assurance.

operator/assurance-level.description: Assurance level of the operator participant at the time the binding is accepted.

derived/node-assurance-level.description: Assurance level exposed by the node for eligibility gates. Consumers MUST NOT treat this as reputation and SHOULD ensure it does not exceed the operator assurance level.

derivation/mode.description: How the node assurance was derived from the operator relationship.

valid/from.description: Timestamp when the binding becomes valid.

valid/until.description: Optional timestamp after which the binding should no longer be treated as active.

basis/refs.description: Evidence references supporting the binding, such as node identity, operator attestation, or federation approval records.

approved-by/id.description: Council or federation approval identity required for reviewed exceptions.

approved-at.description: Timestamp when a reviewed exception was approved.

issuer/participant_id.description: Participant identity of the primary operator issuing the consent passport.

issuer/node_id.description: Node from which the operator participant acted when issuing the passport.

revocation_ref.description: Reference to a revocation endpoint or log for this passport. `null` means no external revocation reference is declared.

issuer_delegation.description: Optional inline delegation proof reused from `capability-passport.v1` when the participant signs through a delegated proxy key.

signature.description: Operator participant signature over the canonical passport payload.

node_acceptance.description: Node-side acceptance proving that the target node accepts the passport issuer as its primary operator. A participant-issued passport without this node acceptance is not a binding.

acceptance/id.description: Stable identifier of the node acceptance record.

accepted_at.description: Timestamp when the node accepted the operator passport.

passport_id.description: Passport accepted by the node. Consumers SHOULD require equality with `passport.passport_id`.

passport_hash.description: Hash of the canonical signed passport payload or full passport artifact, as specified by the runtime profile. Consumers MUST compare it with the presented passport.

node_id.description: Node accepting the operator passport. Consumers SHOULD require equality with `passport.node_id`.

operator/participant_id.description: Operator participant accepted by the node. Consumers SHOULD require equality with `passport.issuer/participant_id`.

signature.description: Node Ed25519 signature over the canonical acceptance payload with `signature` omitted.

published/disclosure-mode.description: Disclosure posture for this binding. `seed-directory` means the node explicitly chose higher availability for the node/operator relation.

seed-directory/ref.description: Optional Seed Directory publication reference when disclosure mode is `seed-directory`.

revocation/ref.description: Reference to the record that revoked or superseded this binding.

policy_annotations.description: Optional local or federation policy annotations that do not change the core binding semantics.

assuranceLevel.description: Identity assurance level recognized by the surrounding policy for participant/operator identity proofing.
